@markschaver Wow, awesome piece. Very grateful you found it useful! Cheers!

I have to say, it is really exciting to see the CEO of Twitter embrace Protocols, Not Platforms. This is the way the web was designed; open, for everyone, with a singular goal of shared knowledge and community. https://t.co/YGqIF4NNS0

don't ask me how I ended up here, but this is a beautiful little piece of internet anthropology where the host interviews the host of a hotel/homeless shelter in an online survival video game – things are surprisingly wholesome  https://t.co/7vSK89wcw0
if anyone has any undercover missions for me let me know
Time for a delicious dinner.
Doing my homework. So much of a backlog from my absences. Par for the course, I suppose.
I'm a whale, I'm a whale, I'm a whaley whaley whale. http://t.co/b7GUsenI
Wandering around... hungry and tired.
But that moment of satisfaction of finally getting it exactly right is only good because of the nervousness of waiting to hear the feedback.
And, I mean, that's my favorite part of design, not to get cheesy. When I get to help build something that makes my client happy (ideally)
There's that little moment when you make something you're really proud of and you send it to the client hoping they don't hate it. Twitchy.
Working feels good.
RT @mrgan: Saw that Photoshop and Illustrator CS6 got updated with retina graphics, ran to my computer, realized I don't own a retina Mac.
On the bus. It's cold, but the beautiful layer of ice on everything truly makes up for it. Should have brought the camera with me.
Because going to sleep at normal times is no fun at all.
my only goal for 2020 is to try and get Nathan Fielder to do a Fresh Air style interview with me

posts.dek: In which the author concludes a successful client project, compiles documentation for informed AI context, evaluates command-running capabilities, plans an EXIF photo printer tool, experiments with Tres JS, and treats wood panels for gouache painting.
